title: August 2024 Monthly Manager’s Report
summary: August 13, 2024 SUMMARY It has been a great summer…but seems to have flown by remarkably fast. I hope you have enjoyed the last half of July when the sockeye fishing was terrific. I would suspect our local businesses also benefited, as the number of tourists on the Peninsula was as high as ever. As we look ahead from a busy summer, we are gearing up for the challenges of a busy fall season as well, which includes the end of our construction season, the beginning of our local neighborhood Community Meetings, Alaska Power Association’s (APA) Annual Meeting in Fairbanks, and the National Rural Electric Cooperatives (NRECA) Region 7/9 Meeting, to name a few. The following are brief updates on HEA’s current business activities and strategic goals that have taken place over the last month. SAFETY HEA staff continue their monthly self-paced online safety training. This pro...

title: Petersburg residents resurrect U.S. Coast Guard Auxiliary flotilla
summary: The U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Pike. (Photo by KFSK) A group of Petersburg residents is bringing back the local U.S. Coast Guard Auxiliary flotilla — an un-uniformed volunteer group that helps the Coast Guard with everything from dockside safety exams, to search and rescue missions, to cooking meals aboard their vessels. Now, the group is asking community members to step up and get involved. The...

title: Petersburg’s OBI Seafoods plant reaches 100,000 cases of fish a little later than last year
summary: OBI cannery foreman Jeff Randrup blows the plant’s steam whistle on Monday, August 12, 2024. (Photo by Olivia Schmidt/KFSK) Petersburg’s OBI Seafoods processing plant hit an important yearly milestone on Monday, August 12, with 100,000 cases of fish packed. title: The Arctic Institute’s Arctic Extractivism Series 2024: Introduction
summary: This series explores Arctic extractivism, expanding the definition of extractivism and what it means for Indigenous and local communities.
